translations.translation:
Yang Jun (Jicai)
楊俊 (季才)
Lived ADca.170–222
aSanguozhiBiographyRecords of the Three Kingdoms [三國志]
Scroll 23 (Wei 23)
byZL181
onJanuary 26th, 2025

Yang Jun, styledJicai, was fromHuojia[獲嘉],Henei[河內].
He received learning fromBian RangofChenliu[陳留], and[Bian] Rangsaw him as extraordinary.
[Yang] Jun, believed that with the rising military chaos,Henei[河內] would certainly be involved in open battles because it was situated as a thoroughfare for four directions. Therefore, he guided and supported the elderly and weak to go reside within Mount Jing [京山] and Mount Mi [密山]. Those who accompanied him numbered over a hundred households.
[Yang] Jungave charity to the poor and needy. All of them would share what they had for what they had not. His clansmen and friends who were kidnapped by people and made into menial servants numbered six households altogether, but[Yang] Junspent his wealth to ransom them all.
When theCommanding Kingof the Sima clanwas sixteen or seventeen, he and[Yang] Junmet.[Yang] Junsaid, “This is no ordinary man!” There was alsoSima Lang, who had a famous reputation when he was young. His older clansman,[Sima] Zhi, was not known yet by the masses. Only[Yang] Junsaid, “[Sima] Zhimay not compare to[Sima] Langin fame and prestige, but he is superior in sincerity and pragmatism.”
[Yang] Junmoved to hide inBing province[并州].Wang Xiangof his home commandery was fatherless and isolated when he was young, and worked for others as a servant. When he was seventeen or eighteen years old, he was tasked with shepherding sheep, but he read books in private, and was therefore clubbed and caned.[Yang] Junadmired his talent and character, and ransomed[Wang] Xiangto go to his household. He betrothed him a wife and established an abode for him. Later, they separated.
TheGrand Progenitorpromoted[Yang] JuntoChiefofQuliang[曲梁長], who then entered to serve as abureaucratic clerkunder theAssisting Chancellor. He was nominated asOutstanding Talent, then becamePrefectofAnling[安陵令], then was promoted to becomeGrand AdministratorofNanyang[南陽太守]. He promulgated virtuous education and established schools, and the officials and peasants commended him. He was shifted to serve asDirector Generalunder theGeneral who Conquers the South.
When theState of Weiwas established, he was promoted toCapital Commandant. When theGrand Progenitorwas in an expedition againstHanzhong[漢中],Wei Fengrebelled inYe[鄴].[Yang] Junimpeached himself and went to theprovisional residency.[Yang] Jun, being exempt from faults, sent a letter of resignation to theHeir Apparent. TheHeir Apparentwas discontent and said, “Capital CommandantYangleft when he could! How can he be this arrogant and lofty!” He received a letter demoting him toGrand AdministratorofPingyuan[平原太守].
After theCivil Emperortreaded the imperial steps, he was assigned to be inNanyang[南陽] again. At the time,Wang Xiangserved asCavalier Regular Attendant. He recommended[Yang] Jun, stating:
“I lay prostrate to testify that theGrand AdministratorofNanyang[南陽太守],Yang Jun, is pure and incorrupt in his outstanding character, and he conducts himself with loyalty and courtesy in his magnanimous qualities. His practice of benevolence is enough to nurture life, and his sincerity is enough to stir the masses. He can grow the underdeveloped, never tiring in his kind teachings. On the outside, he is lenient, and on the inside, he is candid. He is benevolent, but decisive. When he first started his career, he spread moral influence wherever he served, and when he administeredNanyang[南陽] once more, his grace and virtue has disseminated and became renowned. This was such that distant lands and separate factions came to him with swaddling clothes on their back. Now that his governance has brought peace and tranquility, there is nothing left where he can display his genius and talent. He should be brought back to this court where he may devote his power toYour Majestyand bring glory to the imperial undertaking!”
From when[Yang] Junwas young to when he grew older, he took it upon himself to evaluate people.Shen Guof his home commandery andWei XunofChenliu[陳留] both originated from military squads of five.[Yang] Junhad supported, promoted, rewarded, and brought [them to positions]. They would all become fine gentlemen. Later,[Shen] Guheld rank asCommandery Administratorin different areas while[Wei] XunbecameImperial CensorandCounty Prefect. There were many cases where his discernment of the righteous was like this.
Previously, theMarquisofLinzi[臨菑侯] and[Yang] Junwere friendly. The heir of theGrand Progenitorborn by principal wife was not decided at that time, and he secretly consulted the various high ministers. Although[Yang] Junappraised both theCivil Emperorand theMarquisofLinzi[臨菑] as having strengths in their innate talents and had no inclination towards who was most suitable, he praisedMarquisofLinzi[臨菑] as being magnificent. TheCivil Emperoroften resented him for this.
In the third year of theera [222], theimperial chariotarrived inYuan[宛]. Because the marketplace lacked abundance and revelry, he was furious and arrested[Yang] Jun. TheVice Secretariat Minister, who was theCommanding Kingof the Sima clan, and Regular AttendantsWang XiangandXun Weibegged for[Yang] Jun, kowtowing and shedding blood. TheEmperordid not consent.[Yang] Junsaid, “I know my guilt!” He then killed himself. The masses felt this was unjust and ached forhim.A
TheSayings of the Worldstates:[Yang] Junhad two grandsons: [Yang] Lan [楊覽], styled Gongzan [公質], who was theGrand AdministratorofRuyin[汝陰太守], and [Yang] Yi [楊猗], styled Gongyan [公彥], who wasSecretariat Minister. They were the uncles of thePrinceofDonghai[東海王] underJin,[Sima] Yue. The son of [Yang] Lan [楊覽], [Yang] Chen [楊沈], styled Xuanhong [宣弘], wasCavalier Regular Attendant.
TheSummary of Weistates:Wang Xiangwas styledXibo. When he was known and promoted by[Yang] Jun, he indeed had talent and aspiration. During theera, he andXun Wei, among others, of the same commandery were all respected and treated well by theHeir ApparentofWei. AfterWang Can,Chen Lin,Ruan Yu, andLu Cui, among others, died,[Wang] Xianghad the greatest talent among the newcomers.
WhenWeihad the realm under the heavens,[Wang] Xiangwas conferred to beCentral Army Protector, then was promoted toCavalier Regular Attendantand enfeoffed as aRanged Marquis. When an edict was issued for theAugust Overviewto be compiled,[Wang] Xiangwas madeSupervisor of Confidential Depository. Starting in the first year of theera [220],[Wang] Xiangcompiled and gathered [sources], finishing it after several years. It was stored in the Confidential Bureau. It had over forty sections, with each section having tens of chapters. Altogether, there were over eight million characters.
[Wang] Xiangwas harmonious and generous by nature, and he was gentle and elegant in his literary skill. Thus, the capital praised him, commending him as the “Scholarly Paragon”.
Theimperial chariotwas touring the south, and it had not arrived atYuan[宛] yet. An edict commanded that “officials were not to interfere in other commandries and counties”. When the imperial chariot came, thePrefectofYuan[宛令] did not understand what the edict meant, so he shut the market gates. When theEmperorheard about this, he angrily said, “Am I an invader?!” He arrested thePrefectofYuan[宛令] and theGrand Administrator,Yang Jun. He asked the Secretariat, “How many administrators did theBrightEmperorofHankill?” When[Wang] Xiangsaw the text of the edict, he knew[Yang] Junwould not be exempted. Thus, he went before theEmperorand kowtowed, his face actually bleeding. He asked that[Yang] Junbe commuted from death by one degree. TheEmperordid not reply and wanted to avoid him by entering the palace.[Wang] Xiangtugged at the clothes of theEmperor, so theEmperorturned to tell[Wang] Xiang, “I knowYang Junand you, sir, are affiliated. Now if I listen to you, there would be no me. Would you rather do without[Yang] Jun, or without me?!”[Wang] Xiang, as the words of theEmperorwere sharp, pulled back his hands. TheEmperorthus entered, decided the punishment of[Yang] Jun, and then left.[Wang] Xianghated himself for being unable to save[Yang] Jun, and he would fall ill and die.
